name: embeddings
description: Embedding provider integrations for OpenAI, Voyage AI, Ollama, SiliconFlow, and Enconvo Cloud Plan, with model management APIs.
title: Embedding Providers
author: EnconvoAI

commands.name: enconvo_ai
commands.title: EnConvo Cloud Plan
commands.description: Embedding with Enconvo Cloud Plan , learn more : [docs](https://docs.enconvo.com/docs/providers/embedding)

preferences.name: modelName
preferences.description: The model to generate the completion.
preferences.title: Model Name

preferences.name: concurrency
preferences.description: Number of concurrent embeddings. Higher numbers mean faster processing but should be adjusted based on provider's rate limit
preferences.title: Concurrency

preferences.description: The key management provider to use

commands.name: qwen3-mlx
commands.title: Qwen3 (mlx)
commands.description: Local on-device embeddings using [Qwen3-Embedding](https://huggingface.co/Qwen) on Apple Silicon — runs entirely offline through the `mlx_manage` extension. Multilingual, instruction-aware. The model is pulled from HuggingFace on first use.

preferences.name: modelName
preferences.description: Pick a Qwen3-Embedding variant. 0.6B for speed, 4B/8B for quality.
preferences.title: Model

preferences.name: concurrency
preferences.description: Number of concurrent embeddings. Local MLX runs sequentially per-process — keep low.
preferences.title: Concurrency

commands.name: embeddinggemma-mlx
commands.title: Gemma (mlx)
commands.description: Local on-device embeddings using [EmbeddingGemma](https://huggingface.co/google/embeddinggemma-300m) on Apple Silicon — runs entirely offline through the `mlx_manage` extension. Compact 300M multilingual model. The model is pulled from HuggingFace on first use.

preferences.name: modelName
preferences.description: Pick an EmbeddingGemma variant. 4bit for fastest local inference, 8bit for balanced quality/speed.
preferences.title: Model

preferences.name: concurrency
preferences.description: Number of concurrent embeddings. Local MLX runs sequentially per-process — keep low.
preferences.title: Concurrency

commands.name: jina-mlx
commands.title: Jina (mlx)
commands.description: Local on-device embeddings using [Jina Embeddings v5](https://huggingface.co/jinaai) on Apple Silicon — runs entirely offline through the `mlx_manage` extension. Multilingual text embeddings. The model is pulled from HuggingFace on first use.

preferences.name: modelName
preferences.description: Pick a Jina Embeddings v5 variant. Nano for speed, Small for higher quality.
preferences.title: Model

preferences.name: concurrency
preferences.description: Number of concurrent embeddings. Local MLX runs sequentially per-process — keep low.
preferences.title: Concurrency

preferences.description: The key management provider to use
preferences.title: Credential Provider
